1.5.4.en: Venus D driver rewired for Venus E parity. Previous Venus D releases only polled SOC and capacity - all other capabilities were empty tiles. The community-extended Duravolt v1.1 datasheet (scanned on firmware v153) confirmed the Venus D shares most of its 32xxx/33xxx/35xxx/4xxxx register range with Venus E, so this release ports the full poll loop: battery voltage, AC voltage/current/power, energy totals (charge/discharge), internal + MOS temperatures, inverter state, force-mode reads, work-mode + RS485 control gate, backup mode, force-charge target, force-charge/discharge power, max charge/discharge limits, cutoff SOC settings. Full write support is also included: onoff, target_power, target_power_mode, force_charge_mode, force_charge_target, backup mode, work mode, force charge/discharge power, cutoff SOCs. Flow cards (16 conditions, 9 actions, 14 triggers) mirror the Venus E set minus the four alarm/fault triggers. Deliberately NOT included: battery current + battery power reads (registers 32101/32102 return garbage on Venus D even on the working v147 firmware per tester chats), alarm/fault registers 36xxx (Venus D specific alarm bit meanings are unverified), PV/MPPT registers (not documented in the Modbus range - Marstek exposes solar data via the UDP local API only, users needing PV can combine this driver with the Marstek Local API Homey app), and firmware register 31101 (still known to trigger a deaf-after-error lockup on v149). Compose capabilities updated: added the full parity set, removed the four .pv placeholders plus battery current/power tiles that previously shipped as empty. onInit's repairCapabilities cleans up the obsolete capabilities on existing devices with no user action needed. The one known blocker remains firmware v149 broken Modbus TCP - this rewire makes the driver useful for Venus D users on newer firmware (v153+) when Marstek ships it.

1.5.3.en: Venus E driver: (1) new 'Show fault alarms' setting with three modes - 'Automatic' (default), 'Always show', 'Never show'. Automatic turns fault-warnings off for V3 devices and on for V1/V2 devices. Multiple V3 testers on firmware v149 reported spurious hardware-fault bits ('Hardware Bus Overvoltage', 'Overpower Protection', 'Inverter Soft Start Timeout' etc.) firing continuously while the battery works fine physically - cross-checked against the community-scanned register map (v153+) shows our labels are firmware-dependent guesses and the v149 bits do not correspond to real faults. Alarm log lines still fire in all cases so diagnostic data is retained; only the user-visible setWarning and flow triggers are gated. V1/V2 devices continue to raise alarms as before. Users can override the setting per-device if they know their firmware reports alarms correctly. (2) Undefined-value guards on operation_mode / force_charge_mode / user_work_mode - some firmware versions return values outside our known enum maps (e.g. transient states during mode switches), which used to crash with 'Expected string, got undefined' on setCapabilityValue. Now logs the unknown value and skips the write instead.

1.5.2.en: Venus D: SOC register moved from 34002 to 32104. The 34002 in 1.5.0 / 1.5.1 was sourced from a community HA project that turned out to predate Marstek's firmware register-alignment changes - on Venus D firmware v149 it just times out forever. 32104 is the address documented in the official Duravolt v1.1 Modbus PDF and in the community-extended datasheet scanned on firmware v153. Tester feedback confirmed the 32xxx register range works on this hardware. Scaling stays at 0.1% (raw / 10), so a raw read of 500 displays as 50% SOC. If 32104 also fails on some firmware, register 37005 is documented as an alias ('same as 32104') and is the Phase 1c fallback.

1.5.1.en: Phase 1b for the Venus D driver, plus a cosmetic rename and one safety cleanup on the Venus E driver. (1) Venus D no longer reads register 31101 (firmware) on Venus D firmware v149 where it triggers a 'deaf after Modbus exception' lockup that kills every subsequent read in the session. Firmware in the device-info settings stays 'unknown' until we find a register that works on older firmware (v153+ does have 31101). (2) SOC at register 34002 is now correctly scaled by 0.1% (raw / 10) - tester data showed it was being read as raw percent, so 84% SOC displayed as clamped 100% and stored-energy showed 129 kWh instead of 12.9 kWh on a 15.36 kWh battery. (3) The 'Stored energy available' capability is no longer briefly overwritten with rated capacity during static-info load - only the poll loop writes it now, as actual stored energy (capacity x SOC). (4) Cosmetic: the old 'Venus Battery' driver is renamed to 'Venus E' to match Marstek's product naming alongside the Venus D driver added in 1.5.0. Existing paired devices keep working - only the type label in the UI changes. (5) Venus E driver now wraps fire-and-forget setSettings / setAvailable / setUnavailable calls so they no longer surface as unhandledRejection on the app process when the user deletes the device mid-poll. Pure log-noise reduction, no behavior change.

1.5.0.en: Adds a dedicated 'Venus D' driver alongside the existing 'Venus Battery' driver. (The Venus D is sometimes marketed by resellers as 'Duravolt' - same hardware, same driver.) Venus D is a PV-hybrid battery from a different Marstek product family with its own Modbus register layout (30xxx/34xxx/37xxx ranges plus MPPT registers) that the Venus driver was never designed for - field tests showed Venus D devices on the Venus driver could read only the device-name and capacity registers, then time out on everything else. Phase 1 (this release) is read-only: device name, battery capacity, and SOC. Subsequent versions will add battery/AC measurements, PV/solar readings, and control writes one phase at a time with tester validation between steps. If you have a Venus D currently paired on the Venus driver: the Venus driver will now detect it and show a warning telling you to remove and re-pair using the Venus D driver.

1.4.2.en: Minimal-improvements release built on the v1.3.4 baseline that testers confirmed still works. Adds only three low-risk changes on top: (1) every Modbus error log line is now prefixed with a category tag like [TCP/REFUSED], [TCP/RESET], [Modbus/PROTOCOL_TIMEOUT] so support can tell at a glance what kind of failure it is - log-only, no behaviour change. (2) The settings-page register read/write/dump endpoints now go through the device's own ModbusClient instead of opening a parallel TCP socket - fixes a self-collision on Marstek hardware with single-client native Modbus TCP. (3) A leftover duplicate processBatteryHealth call in the poll loop is removed, cutting 3 redundant reads per cycle. Deliberately NOT included from the 1.3.5-1.4.0 series: the per-instance Modbus mutex (caused regressions on some V3 firmware versions), the lifecycle-hygiene close-grace, the pollData abort-on-wire-failure, the safe Homey API wrappers, the keepalive/fast-check loops, and the connection profile system. If 1.4.2 works for testers who broke on 1.3.22/1.4.0, we know the issue was in one of those omitted features.

1.0.1.en: V3 support finetuning and workflow triggering bug fix

1.0.2.en: Added fault states and workflows and insights

1.0.6.en: More V3 tweaks due to different registers

1.0.7.en: Small mapping bug in charge state

1.0.8.en: Power negative inverted to match Homey standard

1.0.9.en: handle missing firmware register for V3 better

1.0.11.en: Removed deprecated set charge power workflow actions

1.0.12.en: flow formatting fixes and build in delay for mode switch

1.1.0.en: Improve connection management for timeouts and retry patterns

1.1.1.en: Added support for bypassmode and extra mode change token

1.1.2.en: Deprecated action card and updated social links

1.1.6.en: Fix on force soc control ui

1.1.7.en: Added ip and slave id config in add wizard

1.2.0.en: Added Homey target_power capability for standard energy management (requires Homey >=12.13.0)

1.3.2.en: modbus package and idle battery bug

1.3.3.en: Diagnostic ringbuffer for work-mode write/read latency, viewable in settings

1.3.4.en: Hot-path optimizations for sustained target_power writes from external orchestrators
